skill.name: plaid-fintech
skill.description: Provides expert patterns for Plaid API integrations, including Link tokens, transactions sync, ACH Auth, balance checks, webhooks, and item error handling.
skill.summary: Plan Plaid bank-linking and fintech data flows with security-aware integration patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md:3 as a weak cryptographic algorithm and SKILL.md:3, SKILL.md:7, and SKILL.md:13 as system reconnaissance. Review found these are false positives from Plaid Auth, account linking, and access token terminology in documentation guidance. No evidence found for scripts, command execution, environment access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Plaid Auth Term Misclassified
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at line 3. The text refers to Plaid Auth for ACH account verification, not cryptographic code or algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is descriptive skill metadata about Plaid API features. No cryptographic implementation, algorithm name, or executable code is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Banking Terms Misread as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance at lines 3, 7, and 13. The text describes bank account linking and token exchange patterns, with no host discovery or local system inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file only contains Plaid integration guidance and headings. There is no command use, filesystem probing, network scanning, or credential collection behavior.

content.value_statement: Plaid integrations often fail around token flow, webhooks, and item recovery. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concise patterns for safer bank-linking designs.
actual_capabilities: Explains Plaid Link token creation and public token exchange flows.
Recommends transactions sync and webhook-based update handling.
Highlights item error handling, update mode, and disconnect webhooks.
Identifies unsafe token storage and excessive polling anti-patterns.
Supports planning for ACH Auth, balance checks, and identity verification.
Points developers toward compliance-aware fintech integration practices.
limitations: Does not include executable code, SDK wrappers, or runnable examples.
Does not replace Plaid documentation or legal compliance review.
Does not handle live credentials, application configuration, or environment setup.
Provides high-level patterns, not a complete production architecture.

use_cases.title: Plan Bank Linking
use_cases.description: Define the Link token and public token exchange flow before writing backend code.

use_cases.title: Improve Transaction Updates
use_cases.description: Move an account aggregation workflow from polling toward transactions sync and webhooks.

use_cases.title: Review Plaid Failure Paths
use_cases.description: Check item error handling, update mode, and disconnect messaging before launch.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Plaid Link Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for bank account linking.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Plaid fintech skill to outline a Link token creation and public token exchange flow for my app.

prompt_templates.title: Design Transaction Sync
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Plaid fintech skill to design a transactions sync workflow that uses webhooks instead of polling.

prompt_templates.title: Review Error Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Plaid fintech skill to review how my app should handle ITEM_LOGIN_REQUIRED and PENDING_DISCONNECT events.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Integration Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Plaid fintech skill to review my Plaid integration plan for token storage, webhook handling, ACH Auth, and launch risks.

output_examples.input: Plan a Plaid Link flow for a new personal finance app.
output_examples.output: The response outlines server-side Link token creation, client Link launch, public token exchange, secure access token storage, and initial item verification.

output_examples.input: Improve a slow transaction import process.
output_examples.output: The response recommends transactions sync, cursor handling, webhook triggers, retry behavior, and avoiding frequent full history requests.

output_examples.input: Check launch risks for ACH account setup.
output_examples.output: The response flags token protection, balance check timing, identity verification expectations, item update mode, and compliance review needs.

best_practices: Use Link update mode when item credentials become invalid.
Prefer transactions sync and webhooks over repeated polling.
Encrypt access tokens and restrict who can read them.
anti_patterns: Do not store Plaid access tokens in plain text.
Do not ignore ITEM_LOGIN_REQUIRED or disconnect webhooks.
Do not treat balance checks as a complete fraud control.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps plan Plaid API integrations for bank linking, transaction data, ACH Auth, balances, and webhooks.

faq.question: Does it include runnable code?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation patterns and review guidance, not a complete code package.

faq.question: Can it replace Plaid documentation?
faq.answer: No. Use it with current Plaid documentation, SDK references, and compliance guidance.

faq.question: Is it useful for production reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help review token storage, webhook usage, item errors, and polling risks.

faq.question: Does it handle user credentials?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation-only and does not request or process live credentials.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: It supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for planning and review prompts.
